Short description: Benchmark prompt-only personas, profile-rich personas, and calibrated hybrids against real survey descriptives and higher-order relationships.
Empirical unit of analysis: Matched synthetic and human survey responses plus modelled relationships
Candidate roles tested: Counterfactual and transportability judge; calibration architect
Key typology question: Should predictive-AI use in surveys split counterfactual judgment from calibration work?
Observable artifacts / traces: Prompt templates, synthetic responses, regression outputs, subgroup breakdowns
Key outcomes observed: Recovery of means, slopes, interactions, subgroup heterogeneity, prompt sensitivity
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Synthetic panels recover higher-order relationships and heterogeneity as well as human data
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Human benchmark surveys are themselves noisy and historically contingent
Why this case has high leverage: It is the clearest case for splitting calibration from broader causal or transport claims.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
